steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The subject is a noun phrase: “Mathematician Grigori Perelman” (singular) modified by a prepositional phrase. Prepositional phrases don't affect the subject's number, so it remains singular. The blank introduces a verb before the predicate phrase.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb form. Is is the only singular choice. Choices A, B, and C all use plural verb forms (are, have been, are being), which violate agreement with the singular subject. That matches choice D — D is correct.
